- Invoices submitted [monthly / upon milestone completion] to [email/portal]

- Expense reimbursement requires pre-approval and itemized receipts
- Travel expenses subject to Customer travel policy (Appendix [X])

### 6.3 Personnel Substitution

Vendor shall not substitute key personnel without [X] business days written notice and Customer approval. Replacement personnel must possess equivalent qualifications and experience.

---

## Section 7 --- Governance and Reporting

### 7.1 Meeting Cadence

| Meeting            | Frequency           | Attendees       | Purpose                          |
| ------------------ | ------------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------- |
| Status Update      | Weekly              | PMs, leads      | Progress, risks, issues          |
| Steering Committee | Bi-weekly / Monthly | Sponsors, PMs   | Strategic decisions, escalations |
| Technical Review   | As needed           | Technical leads | Architecture, design decisions   |

### 7.2 Status Reporting

Vendor shall provide written status reports [weekly/bi-weekly] including:

- Work completed during reporting period
- Work planned for next period
- Milestone status (on-track / at-risk / delayed)
- Open issues and risks with mitigation actions
- Budget utilization (hours consumed vs. estimate)
- Change request status

---

## Section 8 --- Acceptance Criteria and Procedures

### 8.1 Deliverable Acceptance

1. Vendor submits deliverable with completion notice
2. Customer reviews within [X] business days
3. Customer provides written acceptance or rejection with specific deficiencies
4. Vendor corrects deficiencies within [X] business days
5. Customer re-reviews corrected deliverable within [X] business days
6. If deficiencies persist after [X] correction cycles, escalation to Steering Committee

### 8.2 Final Acceptance

Final acceptance requires all of the following:

- [ ] All deliverables individually accepted
- [ ] User acceptance testing complete and passed
- [ ] Documentation delivered and verified
- [ ] Knowledge transfer and training complete
- [ ] All critical and high-severity defects resolved
- [ ] Transition to support/operations plan executed

---

## Section 9 --- Change Management

All changes to scope, schedule, or budget require a formal Change Order signed by both parties.

| Field                 | Required                    |
| --------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Change Order Number   | CO-[SOW#]-[NNN]             |
| Description of Change | Detailed scope change       |
| Impact to Schedule    | Days added/removed          |
| Impact to Budget      | Dollar amount added/removed |
| Justification         | Business rationale          |
| Customer Approval     | Signature and date          |
| Vendor Approval       | Signature and date          |

---

## Section 10 --- Warranties and Representations

1. **Workmanship Warranty:** Vendor warrants all Services shall be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards for [X] months following acceptance.
2. **Deliverable Warranty:** Vendor warrants deliverables shall conform to specifications and acceptance criteria for [X] months following final acceptance.
3. **Compliance:** Vendor warrants compliance with all applicable laws, regulations, and Customer policies.
4. **Non-Infringement:** Vendor warrants that deliverables shall not infringe any third-party intellectual property rights.
